content: just_audio README state model confirms: play() sets playing=true immediately. Audio only audible when processingState==ready. Play during loading/buffering is by design — playing flag is the user's intent (play/pause button), processingState is decoder state. When ready reached, audio begins automatically. Re-firing play() on ready transition is redundant per design. Fork (sagarchaulagai/just_audio @ a6f8db8) play() at line 1090: if(playing) return; sets playing=true via _playerEventSubject.add; awaits AudioSession.instance; if audio session activation fails, reverts playing to false. seek() at line 1346: returns immediately if processingState==loading (no-op). setAudioSources with preload:true awaits platform load + processingStateStream.firstWhere(state != loading) — returns when state is buffering/ready/idle/error, NOT loading.
